statistics.name: points
statistics.description: The number of points scored.

statistics.name: fieldGoals
statistics.description: Field Goal makes and attempts.

statistics.name: freeThrows
statistics.description: Free Throw makes and attempts.

statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.name: minutes
statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.name: rebounds
statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

articles.headline: Karasinski scores 31 and Bellarmine defeats Jacksonville 82-79 in Atlantic Sun Conference Tournament
articles.description: — Jack Karasinski led Bellarmine with 31 points and Brian Waddell was left open for a dunk with 14.3 seconds left as the Knights knocked off Jacksonville 82-79 on Wednesday in the...

images.name: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights
images.caption: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights

articles.headline: Joe Lunardi's men's Bracketology March Madness watch guide
articles.description: Big stakes tonight for SEC (Texas), ACC (SMU) and Big Ten (Ohio State, Indiana) as Champ Week gets going.

images.name: Highlight: No. 5 Florida puts up 108 in win over Mississippi State
images.caption: Alex Condon powers the Gators with 26 points as Xaivian Lee puts up 19 and Rueben Chinyelu records a double-double of 11 points and 16 rebounds to defeat the Bulldogs, 108-74.

categories.description: University of California at Los Angeles

team.description: University of California at Los Angeles

articles.headline: NCAA to require availability reports from tournament teams
articles.description: For the men's and women's NCAA tournaments, teams must submit initial availability reports the night before a game and provide updates two hours prior to the scheduled tipoff.

images.name: Miami (OH) HC Steele: We want MAC title, then advance in NCAA tournament
images.caption: Travis Steele tells Scott Van Pelt the next step after a 30-0 regular season is winning a MAC championship and advancing in the NCAA Tournament.

articles.headline: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights
articles.description: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights

images.name: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights
images.caption: Jacksonville Dolphins vs. Bellarmine Knights: Game Highlights

articles.headline: Steve Kerr, Doc Rivers join 'political interference' letter
articles.description: A number of prominent basketball coaches, including Steve Kerr and Doc Rivers, signed a public letter released Wednesday contending that political interference in universities threatens to undermine college athletics.

images.name: Steve Kerr [1296x1296]
images.credit: Photo by Noah Graham/NBAE via Getty Images

articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: Griffen scores 21, Dominguez 17 and Texas A&M tops Kentucky 96-85
article.description: — Rylan Griffen and Ruben Dominguez came off the bench to score 21 and 17 points respectively and Texas A&M defeated Kentucky 96-85 on Tuesday night.
article.linkText: Griffen scores 21, Dominguez 17 and Texas A&M tops Kentucky 96-85

tracking.trackingName: NCB_One-Play (Kentucky Wildcats vs. Texas A&M Aggies: Game Highlights) 2026/03/04 ESHEET

article.story: COLLEGE STATION, Texas -- — Rylan Griffen and Ruben Dominguez came off the bench to score 21 and 17 points respectively and Texas A&M defeated Kentucky 96-85 on Tuesday night. With Griffen and Dominguez (who have 48 combined starts) leading the way, the Aggies held a dominant 57-39 advantage in bench points. Rashaun Agee had 14 points and eight rebounds and Pop Isaacs contributed 12 points, six rebounds and eight assists for Texas A&M (20-10, 10-7 SEC), which had lost six of eight coming in. Zach Clemence had 13 points. He, Griffen and Dominguez combined to make 12 of the Aggies' 13 3-pointers. Otega Oweh had 24 points, nine rebounds and five assists for the Wildcats (19-11, 10-7), who have lost four of their last six games. Mouhamed Dioubate scored 19 points. Kentucky led 30-18 with eight minutes left in the first half before Texas A&M took charge. The Aggies had runs of 13-0 and 14-0 while outscoring the Wildcats 27-3 over the final minutes to lead 45-33 at halftime. Clemence hit three 3s early in the second half and Griffen scored 11 straight Texas A&M points while the Aggies built a 73-52 lead 9 1/2 minutes into the period. Kentucky slowly chipped away at the deficit but it didn't get within single digits until Trent Noah hit a 3-pointer with 1:36 remaining to make it 86-78. Texas A&M wrapped it up with eight free throws in the final minute. Up next Kentucky: The Wildcats conclude the regular season with a home game against No. 5 Florida on Saturday. Texas A&M: The Aggies finish at LSU on Saturday. The conference tournament runs March 11-15 in Nashville, Tennessee. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
